question_title: What will be the value of "30-Year Fixed Rate Veterans Affairs Mortgage Index" on 2026-02-26?
summary: The 30-Year Fixed Rate VA Mortgage Index was 5.633% on Feb 12, 2026. With ~9 business days to resolution (Feb 26), I ran Monte Carlo simulations using: (1) bootstrap from ~170 historical daily changes, (2) parametric normal with recent 30-day volatility, and (3) parametric with mild downward drift informed by declining 10-year Treasury yields (4.29→4.09 over Feb 2-12). All three methods converge on a median around 5.59%, slightly below the current value, reflecting the mild downward drift in both mortgage rates and underlying Treasury yields. Daily volatility of ~0.044% over 9 days gives an uncertainty band of about ±0.13% (1 std). The distribution is roughly symmetric with slightly heavier left tail from the bootstrap component.

factors.description: Current value anchoring: last observation 5.633% on Feb 12 provides strong anchor for 9-day forward estimate
10-year Treasury declining 20bp (4.29→4.09) in early Feb, suggesting continued mild downward pressure on mortgage rates
Historical daily changes show mild negative drift (-0.005/day) consistent across recent windows
Passthrough ratio only ~0.33 suggests much of Treasury decline already reflected in current mortgage rate level
Daily volatility ~0.044% creates meaningful uncertainty over 9 business days (std ~0.13% for sum)
